titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/170

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=373 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:44:23.300853+00 | translation moa=374 status=live modified=2021-04-06 12:44:28.191303+00

div.p:
. W . dei gratia Rex Scott' Episcopis . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iustic' . Vicecomitibus . Ministris . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue
Clericis et laicis Salutem . Sciant presentes et posteri . me concessisse et dedisse et hac Carta mea confirmasse deo et ecclesie sancte marie de Mel
ros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus terram illam quam habent de patricio de Ridale in territorio de Wittun . per ipsas diuisas que conti
nentur in carta patricii de Ridale quam habent de eo de eadem terra . ita ut quicquid infra diuisas fuerit habeant et possideant imperpe
tuum . ita libere et quiete . honorifice et pacifice . sicut aliqua elemosina liberius et quietius et honorificentius in regno meo possidetur . Dedi enim
eis et confirmaui terram illam liberam ab omnibus auxiliis placitis . Geldis . et ab omni seruicio et consuetudine et exactione seculari . ita quod ego
et heredes mei manutenebimus et warantizabimus eam illis contra omnes homines in perpetuum . saluo seruitio meo de patricio et here
dibus suis . Test' Iocelino episcopo Glasg' . Dauid fratre meo . Walter Olifard Iustic'. Alano filio Walteri . Hug' clerico de sigillo . Hug' Ridel . Walter
de Windeshoure . Walter de berkel' cam' . Willelmo de Mortimer . Rob' de berkel' . Iohanne de london' . Apud lanarch.

William by the grace of God king of the Scots to Bishops, Earls, Barons, Justices, Sheriffs, Officials, and all worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, Greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted and given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, the land that they have from Patrick of Ryedale in the territory of Whitton, by those bounds that are contained in the charter of Patrick of Ryedale which they have from him concerning the same land; so that they have and possess whatever is within the bounds for ever, as freely and quitely, honourably and peaceably, as any alms is freely and quitely and honourably possessed in my kingdom. For I have given to them and confirmed that land free from all aids, pleas, gelds, and from every service and custom and secular exaction; so that I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant it for them against all men for ever, saving my service from Patrick and his heirs. As witnesses, Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; David my brother; Walter Oliphant, justice; Alan son of Walter; Hugh, clerk of the seal; Hugh Ridel; Walter of Windsor; Walter of Berkeley, chamberlain; William de Mortimer; Robert of Berkeley; John of London. At Lanark.
